characters.name: Eurypterids
characters.description: These aquatic relatives of modern scorpions are ferocious predators, but they face competition from the dominant Straight Nautiloids that rule the Ordovician seas. Once the gamma-ray burst strikes, however, things start to turn in their favor… Big Creepy-Crawlies: Eurypterids are six feet long or more, and some are as heavy as an NFL lineman. Dragon Ascendant: At first, the Eurypterids are outclassed as the top predators by the Straight Nautiloids, but the Nautiloids suffer immensely as a result of the mass extinction. Eventually, the remaining Nautiloids are much smaller than their ancestors, and the Eurypterids take over as the main predators once the Silurian begins. Scary Scorpions: Eurypterids are aquatic scorpions, and they are deadly predators which can grow to six feet long on average, with some growing even bigger.

characters.name: Dunkleosteus
characters.description: A gigantic placoderm that is the apex predator of the Late Devonian seas,Dunkleosteusis theT.rexof its time. The Dreaded: As the apex predator of the Devonian seas, this is to be expected. Establishing Character Moment: Its first appearance shows it devouring a straight nautiloid, to show how far the nautiloids have fallen since the days of the Ordovician and how that they’ve been replaced on the top of the food chain. Monstrous Cannibalism: 250,000 years into the mass extinction, two of the last survivingDunkleosteusesin Morocco are seen engaging each other in a fight that ends in one of the giant fish killing the other and partially devouring its body.Justified, as by this point their prey items are becoming extinct and the cannibalism is a desperate last resort by theDunkleosteusto stay alive a little longer.

characters.name: Tiktaalik
characters.description: A strange fish that lives in Devonian Greenland,Tiktaalikhas an advantage that is also an evolutionary breakthrough - this fish can crawl about on land. They use this ability to migrate to new ponds after their old ones start drying up.

characters.name: Ichthyostega
characters.description: One of the first tetrapods, a descendant ofTiktaalik. Amphibian at Large:Ichthyostegais five feet long from snout to tail.

characters.name: Gorgonopsian
characters.description: Gorgonopsians are ferocious carnivores that prey on the other synapsids of the late Permian - until the Great Dying begins, spelling the beginning of the end of their reign. Downer Ending: The gorgonopsians ultimately succumb to extinction in the Great Dying, with the very last gorgonsDying Alonefrom disease and starvation. Fantastic Fauna Counterpart: To lions. They have fur around their necks like a lion's mane and are called the "Lions of the Permian " by the narrator. The Hunter Becomes the Hunted: One of the last gorgons (the top predator before the mass extinction started) is shown being taken down by a crocodile-likeProterosuchus.

characters.name: Thrinaxodon
characters.description: The furryThrinaxodonis an early ancestor to mammals, and is one of the few synapsids adaptable enough to weather the Great Dying. Scavengers Are Scum: Averted, its first appearance shows it scavenging the corpse of aLystrosaurus, and it’s portrayed very sympathetically.

characters.name: Dicynodon
characters.description: A herbivorous synapsid, closely related toLystrosaurus. Downer Ending: UnlikeLystrosaurus, theDicynodonare rendered extinct in the Great Dying. Palette Swap:Averted, as the closely relatedLystrosaurusandDicynodonclearly have different models, with different colors and proportions, plus theDicynodonhaving some fur.

characters.name: Rutiodon
characters.description: A crocodile-like reptile that prowls the waterways of Pangaea. Colossal Croc:Rutiodonis only distantly related to true crocodiles, but it fills the same niche. One is shown stalking aStaurikosaurusand scaring it off. They are ferocious beasts that will even eatother Rutiodonsif they’re desperate enough. Eats Babies: A newly hatchedRutiodonis seen being eaten by a starving adult. Monstrous Cannibalism: Starving adultRutiodonsstart eating hatchlings out of hunger. Never Smile at a Crocodile: Rutiodons are vicious predators, so much so that they'llcannibalizetheir own infants if there isn't enough food.

characters.name: Desmatosuchus
characters.description: An armored herbivorous reptile common in the Late Triassic before Pangaea starts to break apart. As the centuries pass, they start suffering from the reduced oxygen levels, and become easy prey for theStaurikosaurus. Animal Stampede: Early in the mass extinction, they are seen stampeding away from the eruptions, joined by someStaurikosauruses. Dying Race: 100 years into the mass extinction, the decreased oxygen levels in the atmosphere have badly affected these aetosaurs. It’s harder for them to breathe (due toCarrier’s Constraint), fewer and fewer of them are hatching every year (their eggs are in a Catch-22 dilemma, where their shells need more holes to get as much oxygen as possible, but more water escapes as a result), those that do hatch are not growing as big as the previous generations, and in their weakened state, they are easy prey for early dinosaurs such asStaurikosaurus(who have more efficient lungs) that they were once invulnerable to. The species ultimately slowly ebbs away. Endangered Species: They have become this 100 years after the mass extinction starts. Evenbreathingis more difficult for them due to the decreased oxygen content and fewer of their eggs are surviving to hatch with each passing year. How the Mighty Have Fallen: Before the extinction began, herds ofDesmatosuchusroam Pangaea, having no fear of any predator thanks to their strong armor. In their final years, the armored plant eaters, weakened from the changing oxygen level and with more of their eggs failing to hatch every year, become easy prey for the much smaller and more agileStaurikosaurus.

characters.name: Staurikosaurus
characters.description: One of the earliest dinosaurs,Staurikosaurusis a small, fast, bipedal predator. Anachronistic Animal: Did not live to see the Triassic mass extinction. Animal Stampede: A number of them are shown fleeing alongside Desmatosuchus at one point. Dragon Ascendant: A century into the mass extinction, the armouredDesmatosuchusare severely weakened by their primitive lung design being unable to cope with the changing oxygen levels, and as a result, the early dinosaurs such asStaurikosaurus(which have both a more upright posture and bird-like efficient lungs) are able to hunt and take down the once-untouchable giant herbivores. Raptor Attack: They don’t have the sickle-toe claw (and aren’t true raptors), but they nevertheless mostly resemble the classic view of scaly, pack-hunting raptors.

characters.name: Purgatorius
characters.description: This small, squirrel-like mammal is actually the earliest known primate.Purgatoriusis one of the few animals to actually thrive after the asteroid hits. Meek Mesozoic Mammal: LikeMegazostrodonbefore it,Purgatoriusis a tiny rodent-like mammal that is seen as a snack by small theropod dinosaurs.AlsolikeMegazostrodon, however,Purgatoriussurvives the mass extinction and outlives its larger enemies. Sole Survivor: Of all the named animals in the Cretaceous episodes,Purgatoriusis the only one to survive the mass extinction. (Unnamed types of fish and sharks that also survived appear in the episode.)

characters.name: Hadrosaur
characters.description: Social Ornithopod: They, as usual, are seen congregating in herds.

characters.name: Troodon (and Byronosaurus)
characters.description: Troodon(in North America) andByronosaurus(in Asia) are small, fast theropods. Always a Bigger Fish: TwoTroodonare shown taking down a young hadrosaur in an attempt to kill it, but the small theropods are then chased away by aT.rex. Raptor Attack: TheTroodonin this show (never mind thatTroodonis now considered dubious) are completely scaly and look more likeDale Russel’s 1980s model◊than whatthe animal would have actually looked like◊. Not to mention that two of them are shown taking down a hadrosaur that is at least twice their size. TheByronosaurus, using the same model, isn’t any better.

characters.name: Tyrannosaurus rex
characters.description: The iconic theropod dinosaur that needs no introduction,Tyrannosaurusis the apex predator of North America during the final days of the Cretaceous Period. Handicapped Badass: A Tyrannosaurus with a broken leg is able to successfully take down a Triceratops, but it's implied by the narrator that it won't last long. Monstrous Cannibalism: One of the last surviving Tyrannosaurus kills another of his kind in a desperate attempt to ward off starvation. Too Dumb to Live: The individual that recklessly attacks a Triceratops head on gets impaled by the ceratopsian's horns. Terrifying Tyrannosaur: The one and only. However, even it is not invincible.

characters.name: Mosasaur
characters.description: Sea Monster: A massive aquatic lizard nearly fifty feet long and said to weigh 20 tons.

characters.name: Phobosuchus (Deinosuchus)
characters.description: Anachronistic Animal: Died out nearly 10 million years before the K-T Extinction event. Not helping matters is that a group of them were shown having survived the event. Artistic License – Paleontology: It has the wrong skull shape and should be calledDeinosuchus, something that was already widely accepted by the time the show aired.. Informed Species: Looks more likeSarcosuchusthan an actualDeinosuchusdue to having a narrow rostrum with a bulbous tip, when the latter had a more typical crocodilian snouth. Never Smile at a Crocodile: It’s a crocodilian big enough to prey on dinosaurs.

characters.name: Quetzalcoatlus
characters.description: Giant Flyer: One of the biggestReal Lifeexamples, with a wingspan of 40 feet or more.

characters.name: Triceratops
characters.description: The famous three-horned plant-eating dinosaur that roamed across western North America. Temper-Ceratops: One is shown goring an attackingT.rexto death.

characters.name: Velociraptor
characters.description: This famous small theropod lives in Mongolia, preying on herds ofProtoceratops. Anachronism Stew:Velociraptordied out at the end of the Campanian (72 mya), long before the K-Pg extinction. Possible specimens from the early Maastrichtian were known (now identifiedKuruandShri) but not the end of the Maastrichtian (the relatedAdasaurusorLuanchuanraptorwould be more appropriate). Eats Babies: Two are shown eating unhatched eggsof their own speciessix months after the asteroid impact. Justified, however - they were starving as the plant-eaters they once fed on had died out, and them cannibalising their unhatched eggs is a desperate last resort. Feathered Fiend: Despite being much scalier (with only a few feathers on the head, arms, and tail) than it actually was, it still hassomefeathers, unlikeTroodonandByronosaurus. As for the “fiend” part, it’s a fast and vicious predator that the narrator calls “the cheetah of its day”. Lightning Bruiser: Described as “the cheetah of its day” by the narrator.

characters.name: Tarbosaurus
characters.description: An Asian cousin ofTyrannosaurus, native to Mongolia. Alas, Poor Villain: Technically not a villain since it's a predator, but seeing the individual roaring in agony as it is pounded by the acid rain makes it saddening to watch. Palette Swap: Averted. It has a different and more accurate model than the show's Tyrannosaurus. Terrifying Tyrannosaur: Just likeTyrannosaurus,Tarbosaurusis naturallyThe Dreadedto the herbivores it feeds on -not that the mass extinction cares.

characters.name: Titanosaur
characters.description: All There in the Manual: According to concept art and the deleted website, it isAlamosaurus, which makes no sense, since this taxon is native to North America. More likely, it's meant to beNemegtosaurusorOpisthocoelicaudia, which were actually sympatric withTarbosaurus. Gentle Giant Sauropod: Herds of titanosaurs are shown inoffensively wandering the forests in search of tasty leaves.

characters.name: Elasmotherium
characters.description: Misplaced Wildlife: Native to the mammoth steppe of central Asia and easternmost Europe, but here, it's shown inhabiting the steamy jungles of Sumatra.

characters.name: Sumatran Leopard
characters.description: This large feline is the apex predator of Sumatra. Cartoon Creature: No “giant leopard” fossils are known from Pleistocene Indonesia. The largest big cat native to this region at the time was actually a paleosubspecies of tiger (Panthera tigris soloensis). Lightning Bruiser: This leopard combines the size of a lion with a cheetah’s speed and agility. Outrun the Fireball: Some of them can be seen fleeing alongside theElasmotheriumin a vain attempt to escape the pyroclastic flow caused by the Toba Supereruption.

characters.name: Gigantopithecus
characters.description: All for Nothing: The male Gigantopithecus successfully defends his family from two pumas, but not only does he die shortly from inhaling so much volcanic ash, but its highly likely that his family suffered the same fate. Anachronistic Animal:Gigantopithecuswas already long extinct when the Toba supervolcano erupted. Curb-Stomp Battle: In defence of his family, a maleGigantopithecuskills one of two attacking pumas in a single hit, with the otherfleeing. Downer Ending: It is among the species wiped out by the Toba supereruption. Papa Wolf: The Gigantopithecus male that defends his family from two pumas.

characters.name: Puma
characters.description: A type of big cat that roams Vietnam, the puma faces a crisis when Toba erupts, turning the island into an ash-covered wasteland. Curb-Stomp Battle: A pair of starving pumas attempt to take down aGigantopithecus, but the ape is still too strong for them, killing one in a single strike and forcing the other to flee. Know When to Fold 'Em: After a pair of pumas tries to take down aGigantopithecusand one of them is killed in a single hit by the giant ape, the other puma (although it is desperately hungry) realises that it is no match for theGigantopithecusand escapes. Misplaced Wildlife: Played with. During the Pleistocene, pumas were also found in the Old World, with fossils being known from South Africa (Puma incurva) and northern Eurasia (Puma pardoides), so it'spossiblesome species roamed southeastern Asia, but not actual fossils are known. Uncertain Doom: The surviving puma's fate is ambiguous, but its likely that it either starved or suffocated to death from ash inhalation.

characters.name: Cave Lion (Panthera leo spelaea)
characters.description: Misplaced Wildlife: It's shown living in India, even though cave lions are only known from further north in Europe, Siberia and north-western North America. During the Pleistocene, India was inhabited by Asiatic lions (which still survive today in the Gir Forest).

characters.name: Giant Rat
characters.description: Always a Bigger Fish: The giant rats prey on the large cockroaches in the ruined cities, but are preyed on in turn by hawks. Monster Munch: Despite their size, theseRodents of Unusual Sizeare preyed upon by hawks. Rodents of Unusual Size: These rats are as big asdogs. Despite this, they are easy meals for hawks that survived the extinction event.
